CHAT_IMAGE.description: Image tag for the Solaris Engine chat server (agent core + chat surface + HA conversation facade). Leave default unless you're running a fork PoC.

CHAT_PORT.description: Host loopback port the chat server binds on. NPM proxies the public `chat.<publicDomain>` down to this port behind Authelia forward-auth; HA's conversation agent and the gatekeeper reach the engine's /ollama facade on it via host loopback. Default 8787. NB: the servicebay.healthcheck annotation uses the literal 8787 — change it there too if you change this.

CHAT_SUBDOMAIN.description: Subdomain for the chat surface. `chat` matches what the household stack already points at (#1044); keeping it the same so existing bookmarks / family-portal links don't break. Authelia forward-auth gates every request before it lands on the chat server, which then folds the Remote-User identity into the resident uid (no second login).

SOLARIS_API_KEY.description: Server-side bearer for the engine's Ollama-compatible facade: HA's conversation agent and the gatekeeper present it as `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Auto-generated; regenerate from the wizard if it leaks (the post-deploy re-wires HA's conversation entry on the next deploy).

CONTEXT_WINDOW.description: Context window in tokens, used as the chat-compaction cap (#210): a chat is auto-compacted near ~90% of this, and the /context command reports it. Leave EMPTY (or `auto`) to AUTO-DERIVE it at runtime from the live Ollama active model (#235). Set a positive integer ONLY to OVERRIDE that derivation (ops control). Fallback when auto and Ollama is unreachable: OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH, then a safe static 32768.

OLLAMA_URL.description: URL of Ollama's API (host loopback — this is a hostNetwork pod, so default `http://127.0.0.1:11434` matches the ollama template). The Solaris Engine's only LLM backend.

FAST_MODEL.description: The engine's fast model — the household/voice hot path (device control, quick answers) DEFAULT. `gemma4:e4b` (box-measured 3.3 GB resident @ ctx 32768): the effective-4B gemma4 — strong enough to drive the multi-step voice flows the smaller `gemma4:e2b` fabricated (it would claim an enrolment finished without ever calling the tool, #396), while still a fast single-digit-second hot-path model. The tag must be pulled in Ollama (the ollama template ships it). An admin can re-point the household chat at THOROUGH_MODEL from the panel's Model picker (#366); the selection persists and the household profile picks it up on the next turn — but a larger model on the latency-sensitive hot path is slower, so this stays the fast default.

THOROUGH_MODEL.description: The engine's thorough/"thinking" model — "Solaris Gründlich" chats, the admin persona and the night crons (thinks by default). Defaults to `gemma4:e4b` — the SAME tag as FAST_MODEL — so a single resident LLM serves both the fast and the thinking paths. On the box's 16 GB GPU a warm `gemma4:12b` (8 GB) alongside `gemma4:e4b` (3.3 GB) plus Whisper STT + TTS overflows VRAM and starves Whisper (cudaError out of memory → voice fails, #610); keeping one resident LLM leaves headroom. Point this at `gemma4:12b` only if you free GPU memory elsewhere (e.g. Whisper on CPU).

TTS_VOICES.description: The Kokoro TTS voices the panel's voice picker offers (#368), comma-separated; the first is the default voice. The bundled `solaris-tts` image bakes only the German speaker `martin`, so the default is `martin` and a single-entry list pins it. List more voices here only if your TTS bridge/image actually serves them — the admin's pick persists beside solaris.db and the post-deploy converges it into the Assist pipeline's TTS voice on the next deploy.

LOGOUT_URL.description: Optional logout URL shown in the chat sidebar footer (e.g. your Authelia logout endpoint like https://auth.<domain>/logout). Leave empty to hide the logout link.

FRAME_ANCESTORS.description: CSP `frame-ancestors` source list controlling who may iframe the chat. Default `'self'` (no cross-origin embedding). For the ServiceBay maintenance embed (#209) set `'self' https://admin.dopp.cloud`. DEPLOY REQUIREMENT for cross-origin embedding: the Authelia session cookie on chat's NPM proxy must be `SameSite=None; Secure` (NPM/Authelia config, not a solaris change).

DEFAULT_UID.description: Fallback resident identity when no SSO identity is present: the chat server uses it when the Authelia `Remote-User` header is absent (e.g. direct loopback offline test, HA pipeline turns), and the gatekeeper attributes a voice turn to it until speaker-ID resolves the speaker. Default `household` (the household-wide identity).

TZ.description: IANA time zone for log timestamps.

HASS_URL.description: URL of your Home Assistant instance. Defaults to http://127.0.0.1:8123 (reachable because both pods share hostNetwork). With HASS_TOKEN, powers the engine's device-control tools, the prompt-injected entity registry, timer announcements on the Voice PE speaker, and the post-deploy's voice-pipeline wiring.

HASS_TOKEN.description: Home Assistant long-lived access token. Auto-populated from home-assistant's onboarding flow (see post-deploy adoption logic) — leave blank in the wizard. Manually setting a value here pins it and disables the adoption.

ALARM_SOUND_MEDIA_ID.description: The media_id Solaris passes to assist_satellite.announce when an alarm (kind=alarm) fires — HA resolves it and the Voice PE plays it INSTEAD of speaking (timers and reminders keep their spoken announcements). Default `media-source://media_source/local/solaris-alarm.ogg` is the royalty-free tone the skill pack ships, which the post-deploy copies into HA's media folder. To use your own sound (e.g. the Doctor Who theme, which can't be bundled in this MIT repo), drop the file into HA's media folder (DATA_DIR/home-assistant/homeassistant/media/) and set this to `media-source://media_source/local/<your-file>`. If the file is missing, Solaris falls back to speaking the alarm sentence.

JELLYFIN_URL.description: Optional: URL of your Jellyfin media server. When set together with JELLYFIN_USERNAME, the post-deploy auto-installs Home Assistant's `jellyfin` integration via HA's config-flow API, so Solaris can control playback through `media_player.jellyfin_*` entities. Leave blank to skip the integration.

JELLYFIN_CAST_URL.description: LAN/device-reachable Jellyfin base URL for casting music to speakers — distinct from the internal JELLYFIN_URL the engine uses for its own API calls. Leave empty (recommended): the post-deploy auto-derives it as http://<box-LAN-IP>:8096 from the LAN IP ServiceBay provides, so it's durable across reinstalls with no hardcoded IP. Set it only to override that derivation. If neither is available the engine reuses JELLYFIN_URL.

JELLYFIN_USERNAME.description: Optional: Jellyfin username the Home Assistant `jellyfin` integration logs in as. Pair with JELLYFIN_URL and JELLYFIN_PASSWORD. Empty skips the integration (post-deploy fails soft).

JELLYFIN_PASSWORD.description: Optional: password for JELLYFIN_USERNAME. The HA `jellyfin` config flow treats it as optional (blank is accepted for passwordless Jellyfin users). Only used to auto-install the HA Jellyfin integration.

GATEKEEPER_IMAGE.description: Image tag for Solaris's gatekeeper container. Leave default for the base bridge. For speaker-ID set this to ghcr.io/mdopp/solaris-gatekeeper-ml:latest (bakes in SpeechBrain/torch) and turn on SOLARIS_SPEAKER_ID_ENABLED.

GATEKEEPER_PORT.description: Host port the gatekeeper listens on for incoming Wyoming-satellite connections (wyoming-satellite CLI / future satellite hardware; the Voice PE rides HA's Assist pipeline instead).

WHISPER_URI.description: Wyoming STT endpoint. Reached via host loopback because both this pod and ServiceBay's `voice` template run with hostNetwork: true. The post-deploy also registers this endpoint in HA for the Assist pipeline.

PIPER_URI.description: Wyoming TTS endpoint. Reached via host loopback. The post-deploy also registers this endpoint in HA for the Assist pipeline.

OPENWAKEWORD_URI.description: Wyoming wakeword endpoint. Advertised in the gatekeeper's Info; satellites do wakeword on-device, so server-side orchestration is optional.

OPENWAKEWORD_CUSTOM_DIR.description: Host directory mounted into the openWakeWord container at /custom_models and scanned via --custom-model-dir (#456 folds in #407). The post-deploy drops the trained "Solaris" wake-word model (solaris.tflite) here so it shows up as a selectable wake word that the Solaris Assist pipeline pins. Solaris owns its whole voice pipeline; the wake word, Whisper STT and the Kokoro "martin" voice are all Solaris-defined, so they live in this stack rather than ServiceBay's app-agnostic voice template.

WHISPER_MODEL.description: faster-whisper model for the Solaris-owned Whisper STT companion container (#456). Leave at the default and the post-deploy auto-upgrades it to a larger model on a CDI GPU box (base-int8 → medium-int8), which the GPU runs faster than the CPU ran base (box-measured 0.38s vs 0.83-2.86s). Set an explicit non-default model to keep your choice on both the GPU and CPU path.

WHISPER_LANGUAGE.description: Spoken language for the Solaris-owned Whisper STT (#456). Default `de` matches the German household.

VOICE_PE_DEVICES.description: JSON map from short device name to Wyoming URI, e.g. `{"office": "tcp://192.168.1.10:10700"}`. The gatekeeper's `POST /push` endpoint resolves `voice-pe:<name>` against this map. Leave as `{}` until you've paired at least one wyoming-satellite device.

PUSH_TOKEN.description: Bearer token presented on the gatekeeper's `POST /push` endpoint. The listener binds loopback (127.0.0.1); a blank token disables auth, which is fine for that loopback-only default.

GATEKEEPER_MCP_TOKEN.description: Bearer token for the gatekeeper's room MCP server (set_room/list_rooms). The listener binds loopback (127.0.0.1); a blank token disables auth, which is fine for that loopback-only default.

IMMICH_BASE_URL.description: Optional: base URL of your household Immich instance (e.g. http://127.0.0.1:2283). When set together with IMMICH_API_KEY, the photo-ingest adapter (#206) reads the library READ-ONLY and maps assets/faces/EXIF-geo into OKF event/person/place concepts. Leave blank to disable photo ingestion.

IMMICH_API_KEY.description: Optional: a READ-ONLY Immich API key (Account Settings → API Keys). Pair with IMMICH_BASE_URL to enable the photo-ingest adapter. Leave blank to skip photo ingestion.

CALDAV_URL.description: Optional: CalDAV calendar collection URL. When set, the calendar/contacts-ingest adapter (#207) reads the calendar READ-ONLY and maps each event into an OKF event concept (when/title/participants). Leave blank to disable calendar ingestion.

CALDAV_USERNAME.description: Optional: CalDAV username. Pair with CALDAV_URL + CALDAV_PASSWORD to enable calendar ingestion.

CALDAV_PASSWORD.description: Optional: CalDAV password or app token (READ-ONLY access is enough). Pair with CALDAV_URL to enable calendar ingestion.

CARDDAV_URL.description: Optional: CardDAV address-book collection URL. When set, the calendar/contacts-ingest adapter (#207) reads contacts READ-ONLY and maps each contact into an OKF person concept (name/aliases/phone/email). Leave blank to disable contacts ingestion.

CARDDAV_USERNAME.description: Optional: CardDAV username. Pair with CARDDAV_URL + CARDDAV_PASSWORD to enable contacts ingestion.

CARDDAV_PASSWORD.description: Optional: CardDAV password or app token (READ-ONLY access is enough). Pair with CARDDAV_URL to enable contacts ingestion.

SERVICEBAY_MCP_URL.description: ServiceBay MCP control surface URL. The admin persona's servicebay_admin toolbox connects here (token minted by the post-deploy, scopes read+lifecycle+mutate). Reached via host loopback.

SOLARIS_SPEAKER_ID_ENABLED.description: Speaker-ID (#84, #350): resolve which resident is speaking per voice turn from enrolled voice embeddings. BIOMETRIC — stores per-resident voice fingerprints in solaris.db. When on, the post-deploy registers the gatekeeper as the Assist pipeline's Wyoming STT engine so the live Voice PE path runs through speaker-ID; the resolved resident is attributed to the turn via a transcript-keyed side-channel. Requires the solaris-gatekeeper-ml image (see GATEKEEPER_IMAGE); with the base image this stays inert (falls back to DEFAULT_UID). Off by default.

SOLARIS_SPEAKER_ID_THRESHOLD.description: Cosine-similarity threshold (0..1) above which a voice turn is attributed to an enrolled resident; below it Solaris falls back to DEFAULT_UID. Higher = stricter. Default 0.55.

SOLARIS_DEBUG_MODE.description: Initial debug-mode default for the `system_settings.debug_mode.active` row. `true` during build phases, flip to `false` for productive household use.
